# Logo

**Object**

The component for including a logo image.

## Declaration

```swift
object Logo
```

## Discussion

Use the `logo` component to display an image that identifies a brand, company, or publication. (Other components are available for other types of images, such as [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Figure], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Portrait], and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Photo].)

See [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/preparing-image-video-audio-music-and-arkit-assets].

### Example

```json
{
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "title",
      "text": "Apple News Format"
    },
    {
      "role": "body",
      "text": "Apple News Format allows publishers to craft beautiful editorial layouts. Galleries, audio, video, and fun interactions like animation make stories spring to life."
    },
    {
      "role": "logo",
      "URL": "bundle://apple-logo.png",
      "layout": {
        "columnStart": 1,
        "columnSpan": 3,
        "ignoreDocumentMargin": true,
        "margin": {
          "top": 15,
          "bottom": 15
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Images

- **Image**: The component for displaying JPEG, WebP, PNG, or GIF images.
- **Photo**: The component for including a photograph.
- **Figure**: The component for including a figure.
- **Portrait**: The component for including an image of a person.
- **ReplicaAdvertisement**: The component for delivering digital versions of print advertisements.
- **CaptionDescriptor**: The object you use in image components for displaying captions when the image is full-screen.

## Inherits From

- Component
